_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Utilize data structures to organize, sort, and manipulate elements of information
reference_document_element_description: Design and document the enterprise data architecture, including platform topology, data domain structure, integration patterns, and data lifecycle policies.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI DA domain. DA-T-001 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DA-K-003 (DA, cosine: 0.430), DQ-S-006 (DQ, cosine: 0.423).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.2/10 (raw 0.625). Shared vocabulary: [data]. DCWF-only terms [elements, information, manipulate, organize, sort, structures] vs WIDAI-only terms [architecture, design, document, domain, enterprise, integration] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.870 (P=0.874, R=0.866). Balanced P/R — the 11-word DCWF element and 19-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.8%). "Utilize data structures to organize, sort, and manipulate elements of informatio" and "Design and document the enterprise data architecture, including platform topolog" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.450 < STS 0.625 (gap -0.175). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [elements, information, manipulate] and [architecture, design, document] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 8.0%. Shared tokens: [data]. DCWF-only: [elements, information, manipulate, organize, sort, structures]. WIDAI-only: [architecture, design, document, domain, enterprise, integration].
